_comment:
Gyroscope null-rate calibration. Operator sets the vehicle on a stable surface and does not touch it while the autopilot zeroes the angular-rate bias. Procedure is fast (10-30s) and has only one failure mode that the operator can fix: motion / vibration during sampling.

Both calibrationDeclined (autopilot refused to start because it detected motion) and calibrationDidNotConverge (procedure started but samples showed nonzero rates) route to the same operator action — `holdStill`. They stay as separate matchers because the underlying error kinds are distinct in the audit trace, which makes diagnosing flaky mounts vs operator timing easier.

Overall budget is 60s — the procedure itself takes seconds; the cap covers operator setup time plus one retry headroom.
Auto-retries (timeout / noSession / autopilotBusy) are handled by the catalogue's default retry policy on the dispatch itself, so the matchers below only see the *final* outcome.

then.detail: Gyro calibration failed with an unhandled outcome — see the audit trace for the underlying response.
